   137	function toNumber(value: unknown): number {
   138	  const n = Number(value)
   139	  return Number.isFinite(n) ? n : 0
   140	}
   141	
   142	function normalizeRow(row: any): FoodItem {
   143	  return {
   144	    id: String(row.id),
   145	    name_fr: row.name_fr ?? "",
   146	    category_l1: row.category_l1,
   147	    category_l2: row.category_l2 ?? "",
   148	    item_key: row.item_key ?? null,
   149	    kcal_per_100g: toNumber(row.kcal_per_100g),
   150	    protein_per_100g: toNumber(row.protein_per_100g),
   151	    carbs_per_100g: toNumber(row.carbs_per_100g),
   152	    fat_per_100g: toNumber(row.fat_per_100g),
   153	    fiber_per_100g: toNumber(row.fiber_per_100g),
   154	    source: row.source ?? "internal",
   155	    is_verified: Boolean(row.is_verified),
   156	  }
   157	}
   158	
   159	async function fetchAllFoodItems(): Promise<FoodItem[]> {
   160	  const pageSize = 1000
   161	  let offset = 0
   162	  const all: FoodItem[] = []
   163	
   164	  while (true) {
   165	    const { data, error } = await supabase
   166	      .from("food_items")
   167	      .select(`
   168	        id,
   169	        name_fr,
   170	        category_l1,
   171	        category_l2,
   172	        item_key,
   173	        kcal_per_100g,
   174	        protein_per_100g,
   175	        carbs_per_100g,
   176	        fat_per_100g,
   177	        fiber_per_100g,
   178	        source,
   179	        is_verified
   180	      `)
   181	      .order("name_fr")
   182	      .range(offset, offset + pageSize - 1)
   183	
   184	    if (error) {
   185	      throw new Error(error.message)
   186	    }
   187	
   188	    const page = (data ?? []).map(normalizeRow)
   189	    all.push(...page)
   190	
   191	    if (page.length < pageSize) break
   192	    offset += pageSize
   193	
   194	    if (offset > 10000) {
   195	      throw new Error("Safety stop: plus de 10000 aliments récupérés")
   196	    }
   197	  }
   198	
   199	  return all
   200	}
   201	
   202	function formatItem(item: FoodItem, index: number): string {
   203	  const verified = item.is_verified ? "verified" : "not-verified"
   204	  return `${String(index + 1).padStart(3, "0")}. ${item.name_fr} | ${item.category_l1} > ${item.category_l2} | ${Math.round(item.kcal_per_100g)} kcal | P ${item.protein_per_100g} / G ${item.carbs_per_100g} / L ${item.fat_per_100g} | ${item.source} | ${verified}`
   205	}
   206	
   207	async function main() {
   208	  const foods = await fetchAllFoodItems()
   209	
   210	  const md: string[] = []
   211	  const json: any[] = []
   212	
   213	  md.push(`# Audit visible food leaves`)
   214	  md.push("")
   215	  md.push(`Total food_items récupérés : ${foods.length}`)
   216	  md.push(`Top affiché par feuille : ${TOP}`)
   217	  md.push(`Filtre : ${ONLY ?? "toutes les feuilles"}`)
   218	  md.push("")
   219	
   220	  for (const group of GROUPS) {
   221	    if (ONLY && group.key !== ONLY && !group.leaves.some((leaf) => leaf.key === ONLY)) continue
   222	
   223	    md.push(`\n# ${group.label}`)
   224	    md.push("")
   225	
   226	    const scopedFoods = foods.filter((item) => CATEGORY_FETCH_SCOPE[group.key].includes(item.category_l1))
   227	
   228	    for (const leaf of group.leaves) {
   229	      if (ONLY && group.key !== ONLY && leaf.key !== ONLY) continue
   230	
   231	      const visible = sortVisibleLeafItems(
   232	        scopedFoods.filter((item) => matchesVisibleLeaf(item, leaf.key)),
   233	        leaf.key
   234	      )
   235	
   236	      md.push(`\n## ${leaf.label} (${leaf.key}) — ${visible.length} aliments`)
   237	      md.push("")
   238	      md.push(`Scope source : ${CATEGORY_FETCH_SCOPE[group.key].join(", ")}`)
   239	      md.push("")
   240	      md.push("```txt")
   241	      for (const [index, item] of visible.slice(0, TOP).entries()) {
   242	        md.push(formatItem(item, index))
   243	      }
   244	      if (visible.length > TOP) {
   245	        md.push(`... +${visible.length - TOP} autres`)
   246	      }
   247	      md.push("```")
   248	      md.push("")
   249	
   250	      json.push({
   251	        group: group.key,
   252	        groupLabel: group.label,
   253	        leaf: leaf.key,
   254	        leafLabel: leaf.label,
   255	        count: visible.length,
   256	        top: visible.slice(0, TOP),
   257	      })
   258	    }
   259	  }
   260

     5	  METABOLISM: 'metabolism',
     6	  HORMONAL: 'hormonal',

    10	} as const;

    13

    23	    very_active: 1.9,

    25

FILE: tests/api/clients-nutrition-hub.test.ts

     1	import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
     2	import { createSupabaseMocks } from "../mocks/supabase";
     3	
     4	const mocks = createSupabaseMocks();
     5	
     6	vi.mock("@/utils/supabase/server", () => ({ createClient: () => mocks.serverMock }));
     7	vi.mock("@supabase/supabase-js", () => ({ createClient: () => mocks.serviceMock }));
     8	
     9	import { GET } from "@/app/api/clients/[clientId]/nutrition-hub/route";
    10	import { NextRequest } from "../mocks/next-server";
    11	
    12	beforeEach(() => mocks.resetMocks());
    13	
    14	function makeRequest(url: string) {
    15	  return new NextRequest(url, { method: "GET" });
    16	}
    17	
    18	async function json(res: Response) {
    19	  return res.json();
    20	}
    21	
    22	describe("GET /api/clients/[clientId]/nutrition-hub", () => {
    23	  it("returns 401 when no user is authenticated", async () => {
    24	    mocks.setServerUser(null);
    25	
    26	    const response = await GET(
    27	      makeRequest("http://localhost/api/clients/client-1/nutrition-hub?window=7") as any,
    28	      { params: Promise.resolve({ clientId: "client-1" }) },
    29	    );
    30	
    31	    expect(response.status).toBe(401);
    32	  });
    33	
    34	  it("returns 400 for unsupported windows", async () => {
    35	    const response = await GET(
    36	      makeRequest("http://localhost/api/clients/client-1/nutrition-hub?window=9") as any,
    37	      { params: Promise.resolve({ clientId: "client-1" }) },
    38	    );
    39	
    40	    expect(response.status).toBe(400);
    41	  });
    42	
    43	  it("returns 404 when the client does not belong to the coach", async () => {
    44	    mocks.setServiceResults([
    45	      { data: null, error: null },
    46	    ]);
    47	
    48	    const response = await GET(
    49	      makeRequest("http://localhost/api/clients/client-1/nutrition-hub?window=7") as any,
    50	      { params: Promise.resolve({ clientId: "client-1" }) },
    51	    );
    52	
    53	    expect(response.status).toBe(404);
    54	  });
    55	
    56	  it("returns a shaped payload for an owned client", async () => {
    57	    mocks.setServiceResults([
    58	      { data: { id: "client-1", timezone: "Europe/Paris" }, error: null },
    59	      {
    60	        data: {
    61	          schedule_start_date: "2026-05-26",
    62	          nutrition_protocol_days: [
    63	            {
    64	              position: 0,
    65	              name: "Jour entraînement",
    66	              calories: 2200,
    67	              protein_g: 160,
    68	              carbs_g: 220,
    69	              fat_g: 70,
    70	              hydration_ml: 3000,
    71	              carb_cycle_type: "high",
    72	            },
    73	          ],
    74	          nutrition_protocol_schedule_slots: [],
    75	        },
    76	        error: null,
    77	      },
    78	      {
    79	        data: [
    80	          {
    81	            physiological_date: new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10),
    82	            meal_type: "lunch",
    83	            total_protein_g: 140,
    84	            total_carbs_g: 180,
    85	            total_fat_g: 60,
    86	            total_fiber_g: 20,
    87	          },
    88	        ],
    89	        error: null,
    90	      },
    91	      {
    92	        data: [
    93	          {
    94	            amount_ml: 2100,
    95	            logged_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    96	          },
    97	        ],
    98	        error: null,
    99	      },
   100	      {
   101	        data: [
   102	          {
   103	            calculated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   104	            tdee_adaptive: 2450,
   105	            tdee_formula: 2360,
   106	            delta_kcal: 90,
   107	            avg_intake_kcal: 2280,
   108	            weight_delta_kg: -0.2,
   109	            weight_samples: 4,
   110	          },
   111	        ],
   112	        error: null,
   113	      },
   114	    ]);
   115	
   116	    const response = await GET(
   117	      makeRequest("http://localhost/api/clients/client-1/nutrition-hub?window=7") as any,
   118	      { params: Promise.resolve({ clientId: "client-1" }) },
   119	    );
   120	
   121	    expect(response.status).toBe(200);
   122	
   123	    const body = await json(response);
   124	    expect(body.summary).toBeDefined();
   125	    expect(body.trend.window).toBe(7);
   126	    expect(body.agenda).toBeInstanceOf(Array);
   127	    expect(body.energy).toBeDefined();
   128	    expect(body.availableWindows).toEqual([3, 7, 14, 30]);
   129	  }, 15000);
   130	});

FILE: tests/lib/client/ai-coach/adviceRules.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2	import { selectAdvice, type AdviceInput } from '@/lib/client/ai-coach/adviceRules'
     3	
     4	const base: AdviceInput = {
     5	  facts: {
     6	    dayKind: 'training',
     7	    session: { planned: 'Push A', status: 'completed' },
     8	    nutrition: { kcalLogged: 2000, kcalTarget: 2000, deltaKcal: 0, pctKcal: 100, proteinLogged: 150, proteinTarget: 150, proteinShort: false, status: 'on_track' },
     9	    hydration: { ml: 2500, targetMl: 2500, pct: 100 },
    10	    steps: 9000,
    11	    checkin: {},
    12	  },
    13	  trend: { kcalOverDays: 0, proteinShortDays: 0 },
    14	  freedom: 'safe',
    15	}
    16	
    17	describe('selectAdvice', () => {
    18	  it('cancelled session => silent coach_alert, NO client reprogramming tip', () => {
    19	    const out = selectAdvice({ ...base, facts: { ...base.facts, dayKind: 'cancelled', session: { planned: 'Push A', status: 'cancelled' } } })
    20	    expect(out.coachAlerts.map((a) => a.category)).toContain('program_signal')
    21	    expect(out.tips.join(' ')).not.toMatch(/reprogramm|décale|charge/i)
    22	  })
    23	
    24	  it('never emits program-touching tips for soreness (D9)', () => {
    25	    const out = selectAdvice({ ...base, facts: { ...base.facts, checkin: { soreness: 4 } } })
    26	    expect(out.tips.join(' ')).not.toMatch(/baisse|charge|repose-toi|écoute ton corps/i)
    27	    expect(out.coachAlerts.map((a) => a.category)).toContain('recovery_flag')
    28	  })
    29	
    30	  it('3-day kcal over => firm tip + coach_alert nutrition_trend', () => {
    31	    const out = selectAdvice({ ...base, trend: { kcalOverDays: 3, proteinShortDays: 0 } })
    32	    expect(out.coachAlerts.map((a) => a.category)).toContain('nutrition_trend')
    33	    expect(out.tips.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    34	  })
    35	
    36	  it('freedom=none emits zero client tips (still alerts coach)', () => {
    37	    const out = selectAdvice({ ...base, freedom: 'none', facts: { ...base.facts, hydration: { ml: 500, targetMl: 2500, pct: 20 } } })
    38	    expect(out.tips).toEqual([])
    39	  })
    40	
    41	  it('hydration tip uses coach method (gorgées, no 2L bottle)', () => {
    42	    const out = selectAdvice({ ...base, facts: { ...base.facts, hydration: { ml: 500, targetMl: 2500, pct: 20 } } })
    43	    expect(out.tips.join(' ')).toMatch(/gorgées/i)
    44	    expect(out.tips.join(' ')).not.toMatch(/bouteille de 2 ?L/i)
    45	  })
    46	
    47	  it('sleep tip makes no caffeine assumption (D13)', () => {
    48	    const out = selectAdvice({ ...base, freedom: 'extended', facts: { ...base.facts, checkin: { sleepHours: 5 } } })
    49	    expect(out.tips.join(' ')).not.toMatch(/caféine|café/i)
    50	  })
    51	
    52	  it('caps client tips at 2', () => {
    53	    const out = selectAdvice({
    54	      ...base,
    55	      freedom: 'extended',
    56	      facts: { ...base.facts, checkin: { sleepHours: 5, stress: 4 }, hydration: { ml: 300, targetMl: 2500, pct: 12 }, nutrition: { ...base.facts.nutrition, proteinLogged: 80, proteinShort: true } },
    57	    })
    58	    expect(out.tips.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(2)
    59	  })
    60	})

     1	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
     2	import { estimateCaffeineMg, inferDrinkTypeFromFoodItem } from '@/lib/client/nutrition/drinks'
     3	
     4	describe('drink presets', () => {
     5	  it('scales caffeine for an espresso preset', () => {
     6	    expect(estimateCaffeineMg('espresso', 40)).toBe(80)
     7	  })
     8	
     9	  it('detects tea from drink names', () => {

    11	      name_fr: 'Thé vert',
    12	      category_l1: 'drinks',
    13	      category_l2: 'chauds',
    14	    })).toBe('tea')
    15	  })
    16

    18	    expect(inferDrinkTypeFromFoodItem({
    19	      name_fr: 'Café filtre',
    20	      category_l1: 'drinks',
    21	      category_l2: 'chauds',
    22	    })).toBe('coffee')

FILE: tests/lib/client/smart/adherenceScore.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2	import { computeAdherenceScore } from '@/lib/client/smart/adherenceScore'
     3	
     4	const BASE = {
     5	  sessionDates: [],
     6	  plannedDaysOfWeek: [1, 3, 5],
     7	  mealDates: [],
     8	  waterByDate: {},
     9	  waterTargetMl: 2500,
    10	  checkinDates: [],
    11	  referenceDate: '2026-05-20',
    12	}
    13	
    14	describe('computeAdherenceScore', () => {
    15	  it('returns 0 when no data', () => {
    16	    const r = computeAdherenceScore(BASE)
    17	    expect(r.score).toBe(0)
    18	    expect(r.dimensions.sport).toBe(0)
    19	    expect(r.dimensions.nutrition).toBe(0)
    20	    expect(r.dimensions.hydration).toBe(0)
    21	    expect(r.dimensions.checkins).toBe(0)
    22	  })
    23	
    24	  it('full sport score when all planned sessions done', () => {
    25	    // Window 2026-05-14 to 2026-05-20: Mon=18, Wed=20, Fri=15
    26	    const sessionDates = ['2026-05-15', '2026-05-18', '2026-05-20']
    27	    const r = computeAdherenceScore({ ...BASE, sessionDates, plannedDaysOfWeek: [1, 3, 5] })
    28	    expect(r.dimensions.sport).toBe(25)
    29	  })
    30	
    31	  it('full nutrition score when meals logged every day', () => {
    32	    const mealDates = ['2026-05-14','2026-05-15','2026-05-16','2026-05-17','2026-05-18','2026-05-19','2026-05-20']
    33	    const r = computeAdherenceScore({ ...BASE, mealDates })
    34	    expect(r.dimensions.nutrition).toBe(25)
    35	  })
    36	
    37	  it('full hydration score when water >= 80% target every day', () => {
    38	    const waterByDate: Record<string, number> = {
    39	      '2026-05-14': 2200, '2026-05-15': 2200, '2026-05-16': 2200,
    40	      '2026-05-17': 2200, '2026-05-18': 2200, '2026-05-19': 2200, '2026-05-20': 2200,
    41	    }
    42	    const r = computeAdherenceScore({ ...BASE, waterByDate, waterTargetMl: 2500 })
    43	    expect(r.dimensions.hydration).toBe(25)
    44	  })
    45	
    46	  it('full checkins score when checkin every day', () => {
    47	    const checkinDates = ['2026-05-14','2026-05-15','2026-05-16','2026-05-17','2026-05-18','2026-05-19','2026-05-20']
    48	    const r = computeAdherenceScore({ ...BASE, checkinDates })
    49	    expect(r.dimensions.checkins).toBe(25)
    50	  })
    51	
    52	  it('score sums dimensions correctly', () => {
    53	    const mealDates = ['2026-05-14','2026-05-15','2026-05-16','2026-05-17','2026-05-18','2026-05-19','2026-05-20']
    54	    const checkinDates = ['2026-05-14','2026-05-15','2026-05-16','2026-05-17','2026-05-18','2026-05-19','2026-05-20']
    55	    const r = computeAdherenceScore({ ...BASE, mealDates, checkinDates })
    56	    expect(r.score).toBe(r.dimensions.sport + r.dimensions.nutrition + r.dimensions.hydration + r.dimensions.checkins)
    57	  })
    58	
    59	  it('scoreDelta non-negative when today has more data', () => {
    60	    const mealDates = ['2026-05-17','2026-05-18','2026-05-19','2026-05-20']
    61	    const r = computeAdherenceScore({ ...BASE, mealDates })
    62	    expect(r.scoreDelta).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)
    63	  })
    64	
    65	  it('partial sport score proportional to sessions done', () => {
    66	    // Only Mon done out of Mon/Wed/Fri → 1/3
    67	    const r = computeAdherenceScore({ ...BASE, sessionDates: ['2026-05-18'], plannedDaysOfWeek: [1, 3, 5] })
    68	    expect(r.dimensions.sport).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    69	    expect(r.dimensions.sport).toBeLessThan(25)
    70	  })
    71	})

FILE: tests/lib/client/smart/timelineBuilder.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2	import { buildTimeline, type TimelineSource, type TimelineEntry } from '@/lib/client/smart/timelineBuilder'
     3	
     4	describe('buildTimeline', () => {
     5	  const ISO = (h: number, m = 0) => `2026-05-17T${String(h).padStart(2,'0')}:${String(m).padStart(2,'0')}:00Z`
     6	
     7	  it('returns empty array when no data', () => {
     8	    const r = buildTimeline({ meals: [], waterLogs: [], session: null, activities: [], checkins: [] })
     9	    expect(r).toEqual([])
    10	  })
    11	
    12	  it('orders entries chronologically by start time', () => {
    13	    const src: TimelineSource = {
    14	      meals: [
    15	        { id: 'm1', logged_at: ISO(13), title: 'Déjeuner', meal_type: 'lunch', kcal: 620, protein_g: 42, carbs_g: 58, fat_g: 18 },
    16	        { id: 'm2', logged_at: ISO(7,30), title: 'Petit-déjeuner', meal_type: 'breakfast', kcal: 450, protein_g: 28, carbs_g: 50, fat_g: 12 },
    17	      ],
    18	      waterLogs: [],
    19	      session: null,
    20	      activities: [],
    21	      checkins: [],
    22	    }
    23	    const r = buildTimeline(src)
    24	    expect(r[0].id).toBe('m2')
    25	    expect(r[1].id).toBe('m1')
    26	  })
    27	
    28	  it('aggregates water logs into time-of-day buckets', () => {
    29	    const src: TimelineSource = {
    30	      meals: [],
    31	      waterLogs: [
    32	        { logged_at: ISO(7), amount_ml: 250 },
    33	        { logged_at: ISO(9), amount_ml: 500 },
    34	      ],
    35	      session: null,
    36	      activities: [],
    37	      checkins: [],
    38	    }
    39	    const r = buildTimeline(src, 'UTC')
    40	    const water = r.filter(e => e.kind === 'water')
    41	    expect(water.length).toBe(1) // morning bucket aggregated
    42	    expect(water[0].title).toContain('matin')
    43	    expect(water[0].subtitle).toContain('750')
    44	  })
    45	
    46	  it('includes session if present', () => {
    47	    const src: TimelineSource = {
    48	      meals: [],
    49	      waterLogs: [],
    50	      session: {
    51	        id: 's1',
    52	        completed_at: ISO(11),
    53	        title: 'Push Force',
    54	        duration_min: 58,
    55	        exercises_count: 8,
    56	      },
    57	      activities: [],
    58	      checkins: [],
    59	    }
    60	    const r = buildTimeline(src)
    61	    expect(r.find(e => e.kind === 'workout')?.title).toBe('Push Force')
    62	  })
    63	
    64	  it('includes activity logs with custom_label when provided', () => {
    65	    const src: TimelineSource = {
    66	      meals: [],
    67	      waterLogs: [],
    68	      session: null,
    69	      activities: [{
    70	        id: 'a1', started_at: ISO(18), activity_type: 'other', custom_label: 'Tennis',
    71	        duration_min: 60, intensity: 6,
    72	      }],
    73	      checkins: [],
    74	    }
    75	    const r = buildTimeline(src)
    76	    expect(r[0].title).toBe('Tennis')
    77	    expect(r[0].kind).toBe('activity')
    78	  })
    79	})

FILE: tests/lib/client/smart/waterAggregation.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2	import { groupWaterByTimeOfDay, type WaterLog } from '@/lib/client/smart/waterAggregation'
     3	
     4	describe('groupWaterByTimeOfDay', () => {
     5	  const mk = (iso: string, ml: number): WaterLog => ({ logged_at: iso, amount_ml: ml })
     6	
     7	  it('returns zero totals for empty input', () => {
     8	    const r = groupWaterByTimeOfDay([])
     9	    expect(r.morning).toBe(0)
    10	    expect(r.midday).toBe(0)
    11	    expect(r.afternoon).toBe(0)
    12	    expect(r.evening).toBe(0)
    13	  })
    14	
    15	  it('groups by time slot (morning 5-12, midday 12-15, afternoon 15-19, evening 19-24)', () => {
    16	    const logs = [
    17	      mk('2026-05-17T07:00:00Z', 250),
    18	      mk('2026-05-17T09:30:00Z', 500),
    19	      mk('2026-05-17T13:00:00Z', 250),
    20	      mk('2026-05-17T16:00:00Z', 300),
    21	      mk('2026-05-17T20:00:00Z', 250),
    22	    ]
    23	    const r = groupWaterByTimeOfDay(logs, 'UTC')
    24	    expect(r.morning).toBe(750)
    25	    expect(r.midday).toBe(250)
    26	    expect(r.afternoon).toBe(300)
    27	    expect(r.evening).toBe(250)
    28	  })
    29	
    30	  it('ignores logs before 5h or after 24h boundary', () => {
    31	    const logs = [mk('2026-05-17T03:00:00Z', 100), mk('2026-05-17T07:00:00Z', 250)]
    32	    const r = groupWaterByTimeOfDay(logs, 'UTC')
    33	    expect(r.morning).toBe(250)
    34	  })
    35	})

FILE: tests/lib/cycle/cycleEngine.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2	import {
     3	  hasActiveCycleFromBilan,
     4	  computeAvgCycleLength,
     5	  computeAvgMenstrualLength,
     6	  computeCurrentCycleDay,
     7	  detectPhase,
     8	  computeNextPhaseIn,
     9	  getCycleStateFromLogs,
    10	} from '@/lib/cycle/cycleEngine'
    11	import type { CycleLog } from '@/lib/cycle/cycleEngine'
    12	
    13	// ── hasActiveCycleFromBilan ──────────────────────────────────────────
    14	
    15	describe('hasActiveCycleFromBilan', () => {
    16	  it('returns false for Ménopause / Aménorrhée', () => {
    17	    expect(hasActiveCycleFromBilan('Ménopause / Aménorrhée')).toBe(false)
    18	  })
    19	  it('returns false for Non applicable', () => {
    20	    expect(hasActiveCycleFromBilan('Non applicable')).toBe(false)
    21	  })
    22	  it('returns true for null (unknown = assume active)', () => {
    23	    expect(hasActiveCycleFromBilan(null)).toBe(true)
    24	  })
    25	  it('returns true for active phase value', () => {
    26	    expect(hasActiveCycleFromBilan('Phase folliculaire (J1–J13)')).toBe(true)
    27	  })
    28	})
    29	
    30	// ── computeAvgCycleLength ────────────────────────────────────────────
    31	
    32	const makeLog = (length: number | null): CycleLog => ({
    33	  period_start_date: '2026-01-01',
    34	  period_end_date: null,
    35	  computed_cycle_length_days: length,
    36	})
    37	
    38	describe('computeAvgCycleLength', () => {
    39	  it('returns 28 for empty logs', () => {
    40	    expect(computeAvgCycleLength([])).toBe(28)
    41	  })
    42	  it('returns 28 when all lengths are null', () => {
    43	    expect(computeAvgCycleLength([makeLog(null)])).toBe(28)
    44	  })
    45	  it('averages multiple valid lengths', () => {
    46	    expect(computeAvgCycleLength([makeLog(27), makeLog(29), makeLog(28)])).toBe(28)
    47	  })
    48	  it('filters out outliers outside 21–35', () => {
    49	    // 15 filtered → only 28 remains → avg = 28
    50	    expect(computeAvgCycleLength([makeLog(15), makeLog(28)])).toBe(28)
    51	  })
    52	  it('rounds correctly', () => {
    53	    expect(computeAvgCycleLength([makeLog(27), makeLog(28)])).toBe(28)
    54	  })
    55	})
    56	
    57	// ── computeAvgMenstrualLength ────────────────────────────────────────
    58	
    59	describe('computeAvgMenstrualLength', () => {
    60	  it('returns 5 for empty logs', () => {
    61	    expect(computeAvgMenstrualLength([])).toBe(5)
    62	  })
    63	  it('returns 5 when no end dates', () => {
    64	    expect(computeAvgMenstrualLength([makeLog(28)])).toBe(5)
    65	  })
    66	  it('calculates from start/end date diff', () => {
    67	    const log: CycleLog = {
    68	      period_start_date: '2026-05-01',
    69	      period_end_date: '2026-05-05',
    70	      computed_cycle_length_days: 28,
    71	    }
    72	    expect(computeAvgMenstrualLength([log])).toBe(4)
    73	  })
    74	})
    75	
    76	// ── computeCurrentCycleDay ───────────────────────────────────────────
    77	
    78	describe('computeCurrentCycleDay', () => {
    79	  it('returns 1 on the day of period start', () => {
    80	    const today = new Date('2026-06-01')
    81	    expect(computeCurrentCycleDay('2026-06-01', 28, today)).toBe(1)
    82	  })
    83	  it('returns 14 on day 14', () => {
    84	    const today = new Date('2026-06-14')
    85	    expect(computeCurrentCycleDay('2026-06-01', 28, today)).toBe(14)
    86	  })
    87	  it('wraps correctly after full cycle', () => {
    88	    // start May 1, today = May 29 = day 29 of 28-day cycle = day 1 of next cycle
    89	    const today = new Date('2026-05-29')
    90	    expect(computeCurrentCycleDay('2026-05-01', 28, today)).toBe(1)
    91	  })
    92	})
    93	
    94	// ── detectPhase ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    95	
    96	describe('detectPhase (28d cycle, 5d menstrual)', () => {
    97	  it('day 1–5 = menstrual', () => {
    98	    expect(detectPhase(1, 28, 5)).toBe('menstrual')
    99	    expect(detectPhase(5, 28, 5)).toBe('menstrual')
   100	  })
   101	  it('day 6–13 = follicular', () => {
   102	    expect(detectPhase(6, 28, 5)).toBe('follicular')
   103	    expect(detectPhase(13, 28, 5)).toBe('follicular')
   104	  })
   105	  it('day 14–15 = ovulatory', () => {
   106	    expect(detectPhase(14, 28, 5)).toBe('ovulatory')
   107	    expect(detectPhase(15, 28, 5)).toBe('ovulatory')
   108	  })
   109	  it('day 16–28 = luteal', () => {
   110	    expect(detectPhase(16, 28, 5)).toBe('luteal')
   111	    expect(detectPhase(28, 28, 5)).toBe('luteal')
   112	  })
   113	})
   114	
   115	describe('detectPhase (30d cycle, 4d menstrual)', () => {
   116	  it('ovulation at day 15 (floor(30/2))', () => {
   117	    expect(detectPhase(15, 30, 4)).toBe('ovulatory')
   118	  })
   119	  it('follicular from day 5 to 14', () => {
   120	    expect(detectPhase(10, 30, 4)).toBe('follicular')
   121	  })
   122	})
   123	
   124	// ── getCycleStateFromLogs ────────────────────────────────────────────
   125	
   126	describe('getCycleStateFromLogs — no active cycle', () => {
   127	  it('returns hasActiveCycle: false, nulls for phase', () => {
   128	    const state = getCycleStateFromLogs([], 'Ménopause / Aménorrhée', new Date())
   129	    expect(state.hasActiveCycle).toBe(false)
   130	    expect(state.currentPhase).toBeNull()
   131	    expect(state.currentCycleDay).toBeNull()
   132	  })
   133	})
   134	
   135	describe('getCycleStateFromLogs — no logs, estimated from bilan', () => {
   136	  it('estimates follicular from bilan text', () => {
   137	    const state = getCycleStateFromLogs([], 'Phase folliculaire (J1–J13)', new Date())
   138	    expect(state.confidence).toBe('estimated')
   139	    expect(state.currentPhase).toBe('follicular')
   140	    expect(state.currentCycleDay).toBe(7)
   141	  })
   142	  it('estimates luteal from bilan text', () => {
   143	    const state = getCycleStateFromLogs([], 'Phase lutéale (J15–J28)', new Date())
   144	    expect(state.currentPhase).toBe('luteal')
   145	    expect(state.currentCycleDay).toBe(21)
   146	  })
   147	  it('returns null phase for unknown bilan value', () => {
   148	    const state = getCycleStateFromLogs([], null, new Date())
   149	    expect(state.currentPhase).toBeNull()
   150	    expect(state.confidence).toBe('estimated')
   151	  })
   152	})
   153	
   154	describe('getCycleStateFromLogs — with logs', () => {
   155	  it('confidence = learning for 1–3 logs', () => {
   156	    const logs: CycleLog[] = [
   157	      { period_start_date: '2026-05-01', period_end_date: null, computed_cycle_length_days: 28 },
   158	    ]
   159	    const state = getCycleStateFromLogs(logs, null, new Date('2026-05-08'))
   160	    expect(state.confidence).toBe('learning')
   161	    expect(state.currentCycleDay).toBe(8)
   162	    expect(state.currentPhase).toBe('follicular')
   163	  })
   164	  it('confidence = calibrated for 4+ logs', () => {
   165	    const logs: CycleLog[] = Array.from({ length: 4 }, (_, i) => ({
   166	      period_start_date: `2026-0${i + 1}-01`,
   167	      period_end_date: null,
   168	      computed_cycle_length_days: 28,
   169	    }))
   170	    const state = getCycleStateFromLogs(logs, null, new Date('2026-04-15'))
   171	    expect(state.confidence).toBe('calibrated')
   172	  })
   173	  it('uses personal avgCycleLengthDays', () => {
   174	    const logs: CycleLog[] = [
   175	      { period_start_date: '2026-05-01', period_end_date: null, computed_cycle_length_days: 26 },
   176	      { period_start_date: '2026-04-05', period_end_date: null, computed_cycle_length_days: 26 },
   177	    ]
   178	    const state = getCycleStateFromLogs(logs, null, new Date('2026-05-08'))
   179	    expect(state.avgCycleLengthDays).toBe(26)
   180	  })
   181	})

FILE: tests/lib/cycle/phaseContent.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2	import { PHASE_CONTENT, type CycleContext } from '@/lib/client/cycle/phaseContent'
     3	
     4	const PHASES = ['follicular', 'ovulatory', 'luteal', 'menstrual'] as const
     5	const CONTEXTS: CycleContext[] = ['nutrition', 'training']
     6	
     7	describe('PHASE_CONTENT', () => {
     8	  it('has content for every phase × context combination', () => {
     9	    for (const phase of PHASES) {
    10	      for (const ctx of CONTEXTS) {
    11	        const c = PHASE_CONTENT[phase][ctx]
    12	        expect(c.title, `${phase}.${ctx}.title`).toBeTruthy()
    13	        expect(c.subtitle, `${phase}.${ctx}.subtitle`).toBeTruthy()
    14	        expect(c.bullets.length, `${phase}.${ctx}.bullets`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2)
    15	        expect(c.impact, `${phase}.${ctx}.impact`).toBeTruthy()
    16	      }
    17	    }
    18	  })
    19	
    20	  it('has at least 3 bullets per content block', () => {
    21	    for (const phase of PHASES) {
    22	      for (const ctx of CONTEXTS) {
    23	        const c = PHASE_CONTENT[phase][ctx]
    24	        expect(c.bullets.length, `${phase}.${ctx} bullet count`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3)
    25	      }
    26	    }
    27	  })
    28	})

FILE: tests/lib/llm/callLLM.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	// tests/lib/llm/callLLM.test.ts
     2	import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest'
     3	import type { CallLLMParams, LLMProvider } from '@/lib/llm/types'
     4	
     5	// ── Mock Supabase service client ──────────────────────────────────────────────
     6	const mockInsert = vi.fn()
     7	const mockUpdate = vi.fn()
     8	const mockRpc = vi.fn()
     9	const mockSelect = vi.fn()
    10	const mockEq = vi.fn()
    11	const mockSingle = vi.fn()
    12	
    13	// Chain: .from().insert().select().single()
    14	// Chain: .from().update().eq()
    15	// Chain: .rpc()
    16	function makeMockDb(traceId = 'trace-uuid-123') {
    17	  const single = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ data: { id: traceId }, error: null })
    18	  const select = vi.fn().mockReturnValue({ single })
    19	  const insert = vi.fn().mockReturnValue({ select, data: null, error: null })
    20	  const eq = vi.fn().mockReturnValue({ data: null, error: null })
    21	  const update = vi.fn().mockReturnValue({ eq })
    22	  const rpc = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ data: null, error: null })
    23	
    24	  return {
    25	    from: vi.fn().mockReturnValue({ insert, update, select }),
    26	    rpc,
    27	    _mocks: { insert, select, single, update, eq, rpc },
    28	  }
    29	}
    30	
    31	// ── Mock provider ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32	const mockProvider: LLMProvider = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({
    33	  content: 'Voici ma réponse.',
    34	  tokensIn: 120,
    35	  tokensOut: 40,
    36	})
    37	
    38	describe('callLLM', () => {
    39	  beforeEach(() => {
    40	    vi.clearAllMocks()
    41	  })
    42	
    43	  it('returns LLMResult on success', async () => {
    44	    const { callLLM } = await import('@/lib/llm/callLLM')
    45	    const db = makeMockDb('trace-abc')
    46	
    47	    const result = await callLLM(
    48	      {
    49	        systemPrompt: 'Tu es un coach.',
    50	        userMessage: 'Comment ça va ?',
    51	        clientId: 'client-1',
    52	        coachId: 'coach-1',
    53	        chatMessageId: 'msg-1',
    54	        maxTokens: 200,
    55	      },
    56	      { db: db as any, provider: mockProvider }
    57	    )
    58	
    59	    expect(result).not.toBeNull()
    60	    expect(result!.content).toBe('Voici ma réponse.')
    61	    expect(result!.tokensIn).toBe(120)
    62	    expect(result!.tokensOut).toBe(40)
    63	    expect(result!.traceId).toBe('trace-abc')
    64	    expect(result!.latencyMs).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)
    65	  })
    66	
    67	  it('passes systemPrompt + conversationHistory + userMessage to provider', async () => {
    68	    const { callLLM } = await import('@/lib/llm/callLLM')
    69	    const db = makeMockDb()
    70	    const provider = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ content: 'ok', tokensIn: 10, tokensOut: 5 })
    71	
    72	    await callLLM(
    73	      {
    74	        systemPrompt: 'System.',
    75	        userMessage: 'Nouveau message',
    76	        conversationHistory: [
    77	          { role: 'user', content: 'Ancien message' },
    78	          { role: 'assistant', content: 'Ancienne réponse' },
    79	        ],
    80	      },
    81	      { db: db as any, provider }
    82	    )
    83	
    84	    expect(provider).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
    85	    const { messages } = provider.mock.calls[0][0]
    86	    expect(messages[0]).toEqual({ role: 'system', content: 'System.' })
    87	    expect(messages[1]).toEqual({ role: 'user', content: 'Ancien message' })
    88	    expect(messages[2]).toEqual({ role: 'assistant', content: 'Ancienne réponse' })
    89	    expect(messages[3]).toEqual({ role: 'user', content: 'Nouveau message' })
    90	  })
    91	
    92	  it('returns null and updates trace on provider error', async () => {
    93	    const { callLLM } = await import('@/lib/llm/callLLM')
    94	    const db = makeMockDb('trace-err')
    95	    const failingProvider: LLMProvider = vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error('timeout exceeded'))
    96	
    97	    const result = await callLLM(
    98	      { systemPrompt: 'S', userMessage: 'U' },
    99	      { db: db as any, provider: failingProvider }
   100	    )
   101	
   102	    expect(result).toBeNull()
   103	    // trace should be updated with error info
   104	    expect(db._mocks.update).toHaveBeenCalled()
   105	    const updateArg = db._mocks.update.mock.calls[0][0]
   106	    expect(updateArg.error).toContain('timeout')
   107	    expect(updateArg.error_type).toBe('timeout')
   108	  })
   109	
   110	  it('uses default maxTokens 300 when not provided', async () => {
   111	    const { callLLM } = await import('@/lib/llm/callLLM')
   112	    const db = makeMockDb()
   113	    const provider = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ content: 'ok', tokensIn: 5, tokensOut: 2 })
   114	
   115	    await callLLM(
   116	      { systemPrompt: 'S', userMessage: 'U' },
   117	      { db: db as any, provider }
   118	    )
   119	
   120	    expect(provider.mock.calls[0][0].maxTokens).toBe(300)
   121	  })
   122	
   123	  it('still returns result when trace insert fails (non-blocking)', async () => {
   124	    const { callLLM } = await import('@/lib/llm/callLLM')
   125	    // db whose insert returns an error
   126	    const brokenDb = {
   127	      from: vi.fn().mockReturnValue({
   128	        insert: vi.fn().mockReturnValue({
   129	          select: vi.fn().mockReturnValue({
   130	            single: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ data: null, error: { message: 'DB down' } }),
   131	          }),
   132	        }),
   133	        update: vi.fn().mockReturnValue({ eq: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ data: null, error: null }) }),
   134	      }),
   135	      rpc: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ data: null, error: null }),
   136	    }
   137	
   138	    const result = await callLLM(
   139	      { systemPrompt: 'S', userMessage: 'U' },
   140	      { db: brokenDb as any, provider: mockProvider }
   141	    )
   142	
   143	    expect(result).not.toBeNull()
   144	    expect(result!.content).toBe('Voici ma réponse.')
   145	    // traceId is empty string when trace creation fails
   146	    expect(result!.traceId).toBe('')
   147	  })
   148	})

FILE: tests/lib/nutrition/actionable-remaining.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
     2	import { computeActionableRemaining } from '@/lib/nutrition/actionable-remaining'
     3	
     4	describe('computeActionableRemaining', () => {
     5	  it('keeps informative and actionable layers identical when there is no overflow', () => {
     6	    const result = computeActionableRemaining({
     7	      target: { kcal: 2237, protein_g: 153, carbs_g: 260, fat_g: 65 },
     8	      consumed: { kcal: 1000, protein_g: 80, carbs_g: 100, fat_g: 20 },
     9	      profile: { gender: 'male', weightKg: 90 },
    10	    })
    11	
    12	    expect(result.informativeRemaining).toEqual({
    13	      calories: 1237,
    14	      protein: 73,
    15	      carbs: 160,
    16	      fat: 45,
    17	    })
    18	    expect(result.actionableRemaining).toEqual({
    19	      calories: 1337,
    20	      protein: 73,
    21	      carbs: 160,
    22	      fat: 45,
    23	    })
    24	    expect(result.isExtremeCase).toBe(false)
    25	  })
    26	
    27	  it('compensates fat overflow on carbs first', () => {
    28	    const result = computeActionableRemaining({
    29	      target: { kcal: 2337, protein_g: 153, carbs_g: 260, fat_g: 65 },
    30	      consumed: { kcal: 1749, protein_g: 94, carbs_g: 145, fat_g: 88 },
    31	      profile: { gender: 'male', weightKg: 90 },
    32	    })
    33	
    34	    expect(result.informativeRemaining).toEqual({
    35	      calories: 588,
    36	      protein: 59,
    37	      carbs: 115,
    38	      fat: -23,
    39	    })
    40	    expect(result.actionableRemaining.protein).toBe(59)
    41	    expect(result.actionableRemaining.carbs).toBe(63.2)
    42	    expect(result.actionableRemaining.fat).toBe(0)
    43	    expect(result.actionableRemaining.calories).toBe(488.8)
    44	    expect(result.compensation.carbsReducedG).toBe(51.8)
    45	    expect(result.compensation.fatReducedG).toBe(0)
    46	  })
    47	
    48	  it('protects the fat floor when carbs overflow must be compensated', () => {
    49	    const result = computeActionableRemaining({
    50	      target: { kcal: 2200, protein_g: 160, carbs_g: 200, fat_g: 70 },
    51	      consumed: { kcal: 2100, protein_g: 120, carbs_g: 235, fat_g: 20 },
    52	      profile: { gender: 'male', weightKg: 90 },
    53	    })
    54	
    55	    expect(result.fatFloorG).toBe(54)
    56	    expect(result.minActionableFatG).toBe(34)
    57	    expect(result.informativeRemaining.fat).toBe(50)
    58	    expect(result.actionableRemaining.fat).toBe(34.4)
    59	    expect(result.compensation.fatReducedG).toBe(15.6)
    60	    expect(result.isExtremeCase).toBe(false)
    61	  })
    62	
    63	  it('enters extreme mode only when carbs and reducible fats are exhausted', () => {
    64	    const result = computeActionableRemaining({
    65	      target: { kcal: 2100, protein_g: 160, carbs_g: 50, fat_g: 55 },
    66	      consumed: { kcal: 2400, protein_g: 90, carbs_g: 180, fat_g: 54 },
    67	      profile: { gender: 'male', weightKg: 90 },
    68	    })
    69	
    70	    expect(result.actionableRemaining.carbs).toBe(0)
    71	    expect(result.actionableRemaining.fat).toBe(0)
    72	    expect(result.compensation.proteinReducedG).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    73	    expect(result.isExtremeCase).toBe(true)
    74	  })
    75	})

FILE: tests/lib/nutrition/adaptiveTdee.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2	import { calcAdaptiveTdee, linearRegression } from '@/lib/nutrition/adaptiveTdee'
     3	
     4	const DAY = (offset: number, weight: number) => ({
     5	  date: new Date(Date.UTC(2026, 4, offset + 1)).toISOString().slice(0, 10),
     6	  weight_kg: weight,
     7	})
     8	
     9	describe('linearRegression', () => {
    10	  it('returns correct slope for steady weight loss', () => {
    11	    const samples = [DAY(0, 80), DAY(7, 79.3), DAY(14, 78.6)]
    12	    const { slope } = linearRegression(samples)
    13	    expect(slope).toBeCloseTo(-0.1, 1)
    14	  })
    15	
    16	  it('returns near-zero slope for stable weight', () => {
    17	    const samples = [DAY(0, 75), DAY(7, 75.1), DAY(14, 74.9)]
    18	    const { slope } = linearRegression(samples)
    19	    expect(Math.abs(slope)).toBeLessThan(0.02)
    20	  })
    21	
    22	  it('handles unsorted samples correctly', () => {
    23	    const sorted = [DAY(0, 80), DAY(7, 79.3)]
    24	    const unsorted = [DAY(7, 79.3), DAY(0, 80)]
    25	    const r1 = linearRegression(sorted)
    26	    const r2 = linearRegression(unsorted)
    27	    expect(r1.slope).toBeCloseTo(r2.slope, 4)
    28	  })
    29	})
    30	
    31	describe('calcAdaptiveTdee', () => {
    32	  it('TDEE > intake when losing weight', () => {
    33	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
    34	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 80), DAY(7, 79.5), DAY(14, 79.0)],
    35	      avgIntakeKcal: 2000,
    36	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
    37	      windowDays: 14,
    38	    })
    39	    expect(result.tdeeAdaptive).toBeGreaterThan(2000)
    40	  })
    41	
    42	  it('TDEE < intake when gaining weight', () => {
    43	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
    44	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 75), DAY(7, 75.4), DAY(14, 75.8)],
    45	      avgIntakeKcal: 2500,
    46	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
    47	      windowDays: 14,
    48	    })
    49	    expect(result.tdeeAdaptive).toBeLessThan(2500)
    50	  })
    51	
    52	  it('TDEE ≈ intake when weight stable', () => {
    53	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
    54	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 70), DAY(7, 70.05), DAY(14, 69.95)],
    55	      avgIntakeKcal: 2200,
    56	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
    57	      windowDays: 14,
    58	    })
    59	    expect(Math.abs(result.tdeeAdaptive - 2200)).toBeLessThan(100)
    60	  })
    61	
    62	  it('throws when fewer than 2 samples', () => {
    63	    expect(() => calcAdaptiveTdee({
    64	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 80)],
    65	      avgIntakeKcal: 2000,
    66	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
    67	      windowDays: 14,
    68	    })).toThrow('At least 2 weight samples required')
    69	  })
    70	
    71	  it('rounds result to nearest 10 kcal', () => {
    72	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
    73	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 80), DAY(14, 79.0)],
    74	      avgIntakeKcal: 2000,
    75	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
    76	      windowDays: 14,
    77	    })
    78	    expect(result.tdeeAdaptive % 10).toBe(0)
    79	  })
    80	
    81	  it('confidence = low when source is protocol', () => {
    82	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
    83	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 80), DAY(7, 79.5), DAY(14, 79.0), DAY(10, 79.2)],
    84	      avgIntakeKcal: 2000,
    85	      caloriesSource: 'protocol',
    86	      windowDays: 14,
    87	    })
    88	    expect(result.confidence).toBe('low')
    89	    expect(result.confidenceReasons.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    90	  })
    91	
    92	  it('confidence = low when fewer than 4 samples', () => {
    93	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
    94	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 80), DAY(14, 79.0)],
    95	      avgIntakeKcal: 2000,
    96	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
    97	      windowDays: 14,
    98	    })
    99	    expect(result.confidence).toBe('low')
   100	  })
   101	
   102	  it('confidence = high when ≥4 samples + logs source', () => {
   103	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
   104	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 80), DAY(4, 79.7), DAY(8, 79.4), DAY(14, 79.0)],
   105	      avgIntakeKcal: 2000,
   106	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
   107	      windowDays: 14,
   108	      trackedDays: 10,
   109	    })
   110	    expect(result.confidence).toBe('high')
   111	  })
   112	
   113	  it('confidence drops to medium with partial tracked days', () => {
   114	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
   115	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 80), DAY(4, 79.8), DAY(8, 79.5), DAY(14, 79.2)],
   116	      avgIntakeKcal: 2100,
   117	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
   118	      windowDays: 14,
   119	      trackedDays: 5,
   120	    })
   121	    expect(result.confidence).toBe('medium')
   122	    expect(result.confidenceScore).toBeLessThan(80)
   123	  })
   124	
   125	  it('weightDeltaKg = slope × windowDays', () => {
   126	    const samples = [DAY(0, 80), DAY(7, 79.5), DAY(14, 79.0)]
   127	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
   128	      weightSamples: samples,
   129	      avgIntakeKcal: 2000,
   130	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
   131	      windowDays: 14,
   132	    })
   133	    const { slope } = linearRegression(samples)
   134	    expect(result.weightDeltaKg).toBeCloseTo(slope * 14, 2)
   135	  })
   136	
   137	  it('handles rapid weight loss (1 kg/week)', () => {
   138	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
   139	      weightSamples: [DAY(0, 85), DAY(7, 84), DAY(14, 83)],
   140	      avgIntakeKcal: 1800,
   141	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
   142	      windowDays: 14,
   143	    })
   144	    expect(result.tdeeAdaptive).toBeGreaterThan(2500)
   145	  })
   146	
   147	  it('formula: tdee = avgIntake - slope * 7700', () => {
   148	    const samples = [DAY(0, 80), DAY(14, 78.6)]
   149	    const { slope } = linearRegression(samples)
   150	    const expected = Math.round((2000 - slope * 7700) / 10) * 10
   151	    const result = calcAdaptiveTdee({
   152	      weightSamples: samples,
   153	      avgIntakeKcal: 2000,
   154	      caloriesSource: 'logs',
   155	      windowDays: 14,
   156	    })
   157	    expect(result.tdeeAdaptive).toBe(expected)
   158	  })
   159	})

FILE: tests/lib/nutrition/ciqual-classifier.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
     2	import { classifyCiqualRow } from '@/lib/nutrition/ciqual-classifier.mjs'
     3	
     4	function mkRow(partial: {
     5	  alim_ssgrp_nom_fr?: string
     6	  alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr?: string
     7	  Aliments?: string
     8	}) {
     9	  return {
    10	    alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: partial.alim_ssgrp_nom_fr ?? '',
    11	    alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: partial.alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr ?? '',
    12	    Aliments: partial.Aliments ?? '',
    13	  }
    14	}
    15	
    16	describe('ciqual classifier', () => {
    17	  it('keeps rice items in carbs', () => {
    18	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    19	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'pâtes, riz et céréales',
    20	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: 'pâtes, riz et céréales crus',
    21	      Aliments: 'Riz blanc, cru',
    22	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'carbs', category_l2: 'cereales' })
    23	  })
    24	
    25	  it('routes fruits-only groups to fruits and ignores fruit words in snacks', () => {
    26	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    27	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'fruits',
    28	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: 'fruits crus',
    29	      Aliments: 'Abricot, cru',
    30	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'fruits', category_l2: 'frais' })
    31	
    32	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    33	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'fruits',
    34	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: 'desserts de fruits',
    35	      Aliments: 'Biscuit sec fourré aux fruits',
    36	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'extras', category_l2: 'snacks-sucres' })
    37	  })
    38	
    39	  it('splits oils from butter and margarine', () => {
    40	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    41	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'huiles et graisses végétales',
    42	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: '',
    43	      Aliments: 'Huile de son de riz',
    44	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'fats', category_l2: 'huiles' })
    45	
    46	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    47	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'huiles et graisses végétales',
    48	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: '',
    49	      Aliments: 'Beurre allégé 39% MG',
    50	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'fats', category_l2: 'autres-lipides' })
    51	  })
    52	
    53	  it('promotes obvious meat, egg and rice names even when the group is broad', () => {
    54	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    55	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'plats composés',
    56	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: '',
    57	      Aliments: 'Bœuf bourguignon',
    58	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'proteins', category_l2: 'viandes' })
    59	
    60	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    61	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'plats composés',
    62	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: '',
    63	      Aliments: "Blanc d'œuf",
    64	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'proteins', category_l2: 'oeufs' })
    65	
    66	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    67	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'plats composés',
    68	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: '',
    69	      Aliments: 'Galette de riz soufflé',
    70	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'carbs', category_l2: 'cereales' })
    71	  })
    72	
    73	  it('routes composite meals to fast-food instead of ingredient families', () => {
    74	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    75	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'plats composés',
    76	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: '',
    77	      Aliments: 'Salade de pâtes, végétarienne',
    78	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'extras', category_l2: 'fast-food' })
    79	
    80	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    81	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'plats composés',
    82	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: '',
    83	      Aliments: 'Bouillon de volaille',
    84	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'extras', category_l2: 'fast-food' })
    85	
    86	    expect(classifyCiqualRow(mkRow({
    87	      alim_ssgrp_nom_fr: 'plats composés',
    88	      alim_ssssgrp_nom_fr: '',
    89	      Aliments: 'Pizza au chorizo ou salami',
    90	    }))).toEqual({ category_l1: 'extras', category_l2: 'fast-food' })
    91	  })
    92	})

FILE: tests/lib/nutrition/engine/cycleSync.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2	import {
     3	  getCycleSyncAdjustment,
     4	  adjustMacrosForPhase,
     5	  detectCurrentPhase,
     6	} from '@/lib/nutrition/engine/cycleSync'
     7	import type { StryvrmMacros } from '@/lib/nutrition/engine/types'
     8	
     9	const BASE: StryvrmMacros = {
    10	  protein_g: 160,
    11	  fat_g: 64,
    12	  carbs_g: 200,
    13	  calories: 2000,
    14	}
    15	
    16	describe('getCycleSyncAdjustment', () => {
    17	  it('luteal → positive calorie delta (thermogenic)', () => {
    18	    const adj = getCycleSyncAdjustment('luteal')
    19	    expect(adj.caloriesDelta).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    20	    expect(adj.caloriesDelta).toBeLessThanOrEqual(200)
    21	  })
    22	
    23	  it('luteal → positive carbs delta (serotonin/cravings)', () => {
    24	    const adj = getCycleSyncAdjustment('luteal')
    25	    expect(adj.carbsDelta).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    26	  })
    27	
    28	  it('luteal → positive protein delta (anti-catabolic)', () => {
    29	    const adj = getCycleSyncAdjustment('luteal')
    30	    expect(adj.proteinDelta).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    31	  })
    32	
    33	  it('luteal → increased hydration', () => {
    34	    const adj = getCycleSyncAdjustment('luteal')
    35	    expect(adj.hydrationDeltaMl).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    36	  })
    37	
    38	  it('follicular → optimalForDeficit true (best phase for cut)', () => {
    39	    const adj = getCycleSyncAdjustment('follicular')
    40	    expect(adj.optimalForDeficit).toBe(true)
    41	  })
    42	
    43	  it('ovulatory → optimalForDeficit true (peak performance)', () => {
    44	    const adj = getCycleSyncAdjustment('ovulatory')
    45	    expect(adj.optimalForDeficit).toBe(true)
    46	  })
    47	
    48	  it('luteal → optimalForDeficit false (cravings/metabolism up)', () => {
    49	    expect(getCycleSyncAdjustment('luteal').optimalForDeficit).toBe(false)
    50	  })
    51	
    52	  it('menstrual → optimalForDeficit false', () => {
    53	    expect(getCycleSyncAdjustment('menstrual').optimalForDeficit).toBe(false)
    54	  })
    55	
    56	  it('all phases return non-empty notes', () => {
    57	    const phases = ['follicular', 'ovulatory', 'luteal', 'menstrual'] as const
    58	    for (const p of phases) {
    59	      expect(getCycleSyncAdjustment(p).notes.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    60	    }
    61	  })
    62	})
    63	
    64	describe('adjustMacrosForPhase', () => {
    65	  it('luteal → higher calories than base', () => {
    66	    const adj = adjustMacrosForPhase(BASE, 'luteal')
    67	    expect(adj.calories).toBeGreaterThan(BASE.calories)
    68	  })
    69	
    70	  it('luteal → higher protein than base', () => {
    71	    const adj = adjustMacrosForPhase(BASE, 'luteal')
    72	    expect(adj.protein_g).toBeGreaterThan(BASE.protein_g)
    73	  })
    74	
    75	  it('follicular → calories equal or within 50 of base', () => {
    76	    const adj = adjustMacrosForPhase(BASE, 'follicular')
    77	    expect(Math.abs(adj.calories - BASE.calories)).toBeLessThanOrEqual(50)
    78	  })
    79	
    80	  it('calories always match macro math (P×4 + C×4 + F×9)', () => {
    81	    const phases = ['follicular', 'ovulatory', 'luteal', 'menstrual'] as const
    82	    for (const p of phases) {
    83	      const adj = adjustMacrosForPhase(BASE, p)
    84	      const computed = adj.protein_g * 4 + adj.carbs_g * 4 + adj.fat_g * 9
    85	      expect(Math.abs(computed - adj.calories)).toBeLessThanOrEqual(10)
    86	    }
    87	  })
    88	
    89	  it('no negative macro values', () => {
    90	    const phases = ['follicular', 'ovulatory', 'luteal', 'menstrual'] as const
    91	    for (const p of phases) {
    92	      const adj = adjustMacrosForPhase(BASE, p)
    93	      expect(adj.protein_g).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)
    94	      expect(adj.carbs_g).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)
    95	      expect(adj.fat_g).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)
    96	    }
    97	  })
    98	})
    99	
   100	describe('detectCurrentPhase', () => {
   101	  it('day 1 → menstrual', () => {
   102	    expect(detectCurrentPhase(1)).toBe('menstrual')
   103	  })
   104	
   105	  it('day 6 → follicular', () => {
   106	    expect(detectCurrentPhase(6)).toBe('follicular')
   107	  })
   108	
   109	  it('day 14 → ovulatory', () => {
   110	    expect(detectCurrentPhase(14)).toBe('ovulatory')
   111	  })
   112	
   113	  it('day 20 → luteal', () => {
   114	    expect(detectCurrentPhase(20)).toBe('luteal')
   115	  })
   116	
   117	  it('day 28 → luteal', () => {
   118	    expect(detectCurrentPhase(28)).toBe('luteal')
   119	  })
   120	
   121	  it('wraps correctly for day 30 (irregular cycle)', () => {
   122	    const phase = detectCurrentPhase(30)
   123	    expect(['follicular', 'ovulatory', 'luteal', 'menstrual']).toContain(phase)
   124	  })
   125	})

------------------------------------------------------------
FILE: tests/lib/phaseEngine/signals.test.ts
------------------------------------------------------------
     1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2	import {
     3	  normalizeBodyCompositionSignals,
     4	  normalizeBehaviorSignals,
     5	  normalizePerformanceSignals,
     6	  normalizeRecoverySignals,
     7	  buildDerivedSignals,
     8	} from '@/lib/coach/phaseEngine/signals'
     9	import { makeExerciseRow, makeRawInput } from './testFixtures'
    10	
    11	const baseInput = makeRawInput()
    12	
    13	describe('normalizeBodyCompositionSignals', () => {
    14	  it('returns low confidence with < 2 weight points', () => {
    15	    const result = normalizeBodyCompositionSignals({
    16	      ...baseInput,
    17	      weightSeries: [{ date: '2026-05-01', value: 80 }],
    18	    })
    19	    expect(result.weightTrend.confidence).toBeLessThan(0.4)
    20	  })
    21	
    22	  it('detects weight loss trend with sufficient points', () => {
    23	    const result = normalizeBodyCompositionSignals({
    24	      ...baseInput,
    25	      weightSeries: [
    26	        { date: '2026-04-01', value: 82 },
    27	        { date: '2026-04-15', value: 81 },
    28	        { date: '2026-05-01', value: 80 },
    29	        { date: '2026-05-15', value: 79 },
    30	      ],
    31	    })
    32	    expect(result.weightTrend.value).toBeLessThan(0)
    33	    expect(result.weightTrend.confidence).toBeGreaterThan(0.5)
    34	  })
    35	
    36	  it('returns null waistTrend when no waist data', () => {
    37	    const result = normalizeBodyCompositionSignals(baseInput)
    38	    expect(result.waistTrend).toBeNull()
    39	  })
    40	})
    41	
    42	describe('normalizeBehaviorSignals', () => {
    43	  it('returns zero adherence with no data', () => {
    44	    const result = normalizeBehaviorSignals(baseInput)
    45	    expect(result.adherenceScore).toBe(0)
    46	  })
    47	
    48	  it('returns full adherence with 100% response rate and full sessions', () => {
    49	    const result = normalizeBehaviorSignals({
    50	      ...baseInput,
    51	      checkinResponseRate: 100,
    52	      performance: { ...baseInput.performance, sessionsCount: 12, weeklyFrequency: 3 },
    53	    })
    54	    expect(result.adherenceScore).toBeGreaterThan(0.85)
    55	  })
    56	})
    57	
    58	describe('normalizePerformanceSignals', () => {
    59	  it('returns low performance with stagnating exercises', () => {
    60	    const result = normalizePerformanceSignals({
    61	      ...baseInput,
    62	      performance: {
    63	        ...baseInput.performance,
    64	        sessionsCount: 4,
    65	        exercises: [
    66	          makeExerciseRow({ completion_rate: 0.9, avg_rir: 3, stagnation: true }),
    67	          makeExerciseRow({ exercise_id: 'ex-2', exercise_name: 'Row', completion_rate: 0.85, avg_rir: 3, stagnation: true }),
    68	        ],
    69	      },
    70	    })
    71	    expect(result.performanceTrend.value).toBeLessThan(0)
    72	  })
    73	
    74	  it('returns high performance with progressive overload', () => {
    75	    const result = normalizePerformanceSignals({
    76	      ...baseInput,
    77	      performance: {
    78	        ...baseInput.performance,
    79	        sessionsCount: 8,
    80	        exercises: [
    81	          makeExerciseRow({ completion_rate: 0.95, avg_rir: 2, overloads_last_4_weeks: 3 }),
    82	          makeExerciseRow({ exercise_id: 'ex-2', exercise_name: 'Bench', completion_rate: 0.9, avg_rir: 2, overloads_last_4_weeks: 2 }),
    83	        ],
    84	      },
    85	    })
    86	    expect(result.performanceTrend.value).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    87	  })
    88	
    89	  it('does not crush performance on low RIR when load is progressing', () => {
    90	    const stagnant = normalizePerformanceSignals(
    91	      makeRawInput({
    92	        performance: {
    93	          sessionsCount: 8,
    94	          global_overreaching: false,
    95	          weeklyFrequency: 3,
    96	          exercises: [
    97	            makeExerciseRow({ avg_rir: 0.5, prescribed_rir: 0, load_progressing: false, stagnation: true }),
    98	          ],
    99	        },
   100	      }),
   101	    )
   102	    const progressing = normalizePerformanceSignals(
   103	      makeRawInput({
   104	        performance: {
   105	          sessionsCount: 8,
   106	          global_overreaching: false,
   107	          weeklyFrequency: 3,
   108	          exercises: [
   109	            makeExerciseRow({
   110	              avg_rir: 0.5,
   111	              prescribed_rir: 0,
   112	              intentional_intensity: true,
   113	              load_progressing: true,
   114	              stagnation: false,
   115	            }),
   116	          ],
   117	        },
   118	      }),
   119	    )
   120	    expect(progressing.performanceTrend.value).toBeGreaterThan(stagnant.performanceTrend.value)
   121	  })
   122	})
   123	
   124	describe('normalizeRecoverySignals', () => {
   125	  it('returns poor recovery with high stress and soreness', () => {
   126	    const result = normalizeRecoverySignals({
   127	      ...baseInput,
   128	      checkin: { energy: 1, sleep_quality: 2, sleep_duration: 5, stress: 5, muscle_soreness: 5 },
   129	    })
   130	    expect(result.recoveryScore).toBeLessThan(0.4)
   131	  })
   132	
   133	  it('returns good recovery with optimal signals', () => {
   134	    const result = normalizeRecoverySignals({
   135	      ...baseInput,
   136	      checkin: { energy: 5, sleep_quality: 4, sleep_duration: 8, stress: 1, muscle_soreness: 1 },
   137	    })
   138	    expect(result.recoveryScore).toBeGreaterThan(0.75)
   139	  })
   140	
   141	  it('treats sleep_quality 4/4 as fully optimal', () => {
   142	    const result = normalizeRecoverySignals({
   143	      ...baseInput,
   144	      checkin: { sleep_quality: 4 },
   145	    })
   146	    expect(result.sleepScore).toBe(1)
   147	    expect(result.recoveryScore).toBe(1)
   148	  })
   149	})
   150	
   151	describe('buildDerivedSignals', () => {
   152	  it('returns minimal dataQuality with no data', () => {
   153	    const result = buildDerivedSignals(baseInput)
   154	    expect(result.dataQuality).toBe('minimal')
   155	    expect(result.insufficientData).toBe(true)
   156	  })
   157	
   158	  it('catabolicRisk increases when weight drops fast + fatigue high', () => {
   159	    const daysAgo = (n: number) => {
   160	      const d = new Date()
   161	      d.setDate(d.getDate() - n)
   162	      return d.toISOString().slice(0, 10)
   163	    }
   164	    const result = buildDerivedSignals({
   165	      ...baseInput,
   166	      weightSeries: [
   167	        { date: daysAgo(21), value: 85 },
   168	        { date: daysAgo(14), value: 83.5 },
   169	        { date: daysAgo(7), value: 82 },
   170	        { date: daysAgo(0), value: 80.5 },
   171	      ],
   172	      leanMassSeries: [
   173	        { date: daysAgo(21), value: 70 },
   174	        { date: daysAgo(14), value: 69.5 },
   175	        { date: daysAgo(7), value: 69 },
   176	        { date: daysAgo(0), value: 68.5 },
   177	      ],
   178	      checkin: { energy: 1, sleep_quality: 2, stress: 5, muscle_soreness: 5, sleep_duration: 5 },
   179	      checkinResponseRate: 80,
   180	    })
   181	    expect(result.catabolicRisk.value).toBeGreaterThan(0.4)
   182	  })
   183	
   184	  it('uses anchorDate for signal freshness instead of wall-clock today', () => {
   185	    const result = buildDerivedSignals({
   186	      ...baseInput,
   187	      anchorDate: '2026-05-29',
   188	      weightSeries: [
   189	        { date: '2026-05-15', value: 80 },
   190	        { date: '2026-05-29', value: 79.5 },
   191	      ],
   192	    })
   193	
   194	    expect(result.weightTrend.confidence).toBeGreaterThan(0.4)
   195	    expect(result.dataCoverage).toBeGreaterThan(0)
   196	  })
   197	
   198	  it('anabolicPotential increases with good recovery + performance', () => {
   199	    const result = buildDerivedSignals({
   200	      ...baseInput,
   201	      checkin: { energy: 5, sleep_quality: 4, sleep_duration: 8, stress: 1, muscle_soreness: 1 },
   202	      checkinResponseRate: 100,
   203	      performance: {
   204	        exercises: [makeExerciseRow({ completion_rate: 0.95, overloads_last_4_weeks: 3 })],
   205	        global_overreaching: false,
   206	        sessionsCount: 12,
   207	        weeklyFrequency: 3,
   208	      },
   209	    })
   210	    expect(result.anabolicPotential.value).toBeGreaterThan(0.5)
   211	  })
   212	
   213	  it('dampens subjective fatigue when progression events are active', () => {
   214	    const baseline = buildDerivedSignals(
   215	      makeRawInput({
   216	        checkin: { energy: 2, sleep_quality: 2, stress: 4, muscle_soreness: 4, sleep_duration: 6 },
   217	        checkinResponseRate: 80,
   218	      }),
   219	    )
   220	    const withProgression = buildDerivedSignals(
   221	      makeRawInput({
   222	        checkin: { energy: 2, sleep_quality: 2, stress: 4, muscle_soreness: 4, sleep_duration: 6 },
   223	        checkinResponseRate: 80,
   224	        progression: {
   225	          overloadEventCount: 3,
   226	          compoundOneRmImproving: true,
   227	          recentPrDetected: true,
   228	        },
   229	      }),
   230	    )
   231	    expect(withProgression.fatigueIndex.value).toBeLessThan(baseline.fatigueIndex.value)
   232	  })
   233	})
   234	
   235	describe('buildDerivedSignals RHR and CNS Overload', () => {
   236	  it('computes rhrDelta correctly with sufficient RHR points', () => {
   237	    const daysAgo = (n: number) => {
   238	      const d = new Date()
   239	      d.setDate(d.getDate() - n)
   240	      return d.toISOString().slice(0, 10)
   241	    }
   242	
   243	    const rhrSeries = [
   244	      // 21 baseline points around 60 bpm
   245	      ...Array.from({ length: 21 }, (_, i) => ({
   246	        date: daysAgo(i + 3),
   247	        value: 60,
   248	      })),
   249	      // 3 acute points around 66 bpm (10% increase, >8%)
   250	      { date: daysAgo(2), value: 66 },
   251	      { date: daysAgo(1), value: 66 },
   252	      { date: daysAgo(0), value: 66 },
   253	    ]
   254	
   255	    const result = buildDerivedSignals(
   256	      makeRawInput({
   257	        rhrSeries,
   258	        anchorDate: daysAgo(0),
   259	        checkin: { energy: 4, sleep_quality: 4, sleep_duration: 8, stress: 1, muscle_soreness: 1 },
   260	      })
